hero.headline: LPU Online MBA in Banking and Financial Services: Syllabus, Fees and Career Outcomes
hero.sub: Lovely Professional University's UGC-DEB approved online MBA with a Banking and Financial Services specialisation. NIRF #31 University, NAAC A+. Rs 2,00,000 standard fee, Rs 1,61,600 with 20% Student Grant. Zero-cost 24-month EMI at Rs 6,733/month. Corporate Banking, Financial Statement Analysis, Investment Banking, Management of Financial Services, and Risk Management in Sem 3-4.

sections.tldr: LPU Online MBA Banking and Financial Services: NIRF #31, NAAC A+, UGC-DEB approved, Rs 2L standard or Rs 1.61L with 20% grant, zero-cost 24-month EMI. BFSI depth with Corporate Banking, Financial Statement Analysis, International Banking and Forex Management, Investment Banking, Management of Financial Services, and Risk Management. Strong for banking, NBFC, and financial services careers.

about.body:
The LPU Online MBA in Banking and Financial Services is offered by Lovely Professional University's online division. LPU holds NAAC A+ accreditation, NIRF #31 University ranking (2025), and NIRF #44 in Management. The online programme is UGC-DEB approved.

The Banking and Financial Services specialisation delivers dedicated Sem 3-4 subjects covering Corporate Banking, Financial Statement Analysis and Credit Management, International Banking and Forex Management, Investment Banking, Management of Financial Services, and Risk Management. These subjects are designed for banking professionals targeting credit, treasury, investment banking, and risk management roles.

The BFSI sector is one of India's largest graduate employment sectors with over 1.5 million employees in scheduled commercial banks alone. LPU's NIRF #31 ranking and NAAC A+ accreditation combined with the lowest zero-cost EMI rate among NIRF-ranked online MBA programmes makes this the most accessible BFSI-focused management credential for working banking professionals.

sem3Subjects.name: Corporate Banking
sem3Subjects.description: Manage corporate credit assessment, loan structuring, working capital finance, and corporate treasury relationships.

sem3Subjects.name: Financial Statement Analysis and Credit Management
sem3Subjects.description: Conduct financial statement analysis for credit appraisal, assess creditworthiness, and manage non-performing asset resolution.

sem3Subjects.name: International Banking and Forex Management
sem3Subjects.description: Manage correspondent banking operations, FOREX treasury, trade finance instruments, and cross-border payment systems.

sem3Subjects.name: Corporate Strategy and Entrepreneurship
sem3Subjects.description: Apply strategic frameworks to banking business models, fintech disruption strategy, and financial services growth planning.

sem3Subjects.name: Generic Elective I
sem3Subjects.description: Elective subject from the GE basket chosen by the student to complement the Banking and Financial Services specialisation.

sem3Subjects.name: Seminar on Summer Training
sem3Subjects.description: Present summer training findings from banking or financial services placement to faculty panel.

sem4Subjects.name: Capstone Project
sem4Subjects.description: Complete an applied BFSI project on credit risk management, investment strategy, or financial services product design.

sem4Subjects.name: Investment Banking
sem4Subjects.description: Apply DCF and comparable company valuation, structure M&A deals, and manage IPO and debt capital market transactions.

sem4Subjects.name: Management of Financial Services
sem4Subjects.description: Manage mutual fund operations, insurance product distribution, wealth management advisory, and NBFC service delivery.

sem4Subjects.name: Risk Management
sem4Subjects.description: Identify, quantify, and manage credit risk, market risk, operational risk, and liquidity risk in banking and financial institutions.

sem4Subjects.name: Generic Elective II
sem4Subjects.description: Elective subject from the GE basket chosen by the student to complement the Banking and Financial Services specialisation.

syllabus.note: Sem 1-2 shared core subjects across all LPU Online MBA specialisations. See the main LPU Online MBA page for core subject list.

whoHires.body:
Banking and Financial Services MBA graduates from LPU Online are hired across these industries and roles:

**Industries:** Commercial and retail banking, Investment banking, Insurance and wealth management, NBFCs and HFCs, Fintech startups, Financial consulting

**Entry-level roles:** Credit Analyst, Relationship Manager (Banking), Risk Analyst, Trade Finance Executive, Investment Research Analyst

**Mid-level roles (3-5 years experience):** Branch Manager, Credit Manager, Risk Manager, Relationship Manager (Corporate), Investment Banking Analyst

**Senior roles (7+ years):** VP Banking, Regional Credit Head, Head of Risk, Investment Banking Vice President, Chief Risk Officer

**Top hiring organisations:** SBI, HDFC Bank, ICICI Bank, Kotak Mahindra Bank, Axis Bank, Yes Bank, Bajaj Finance, Muthoot Finance, IIFL Finance, EXIM Bank, RBL Bank, IndusInd Bank, Edelweiss Financial Services, Motilal Oswal

skills.body:
The LPU Banking and Financial Services specialisation builds skills that banking employers test at credit manager and relationship manager levels:

**Technical skills:**
- Financial Statement Analysis and Credit Management: credit appraisal from balance sheet analysis, NPA resolution frameworks, and creditworthiness assessment for credit analyst and credit manager roles
- Corporate Banking: working capital finance structuring, term loan assessment, and corporate treasury relationship management for corporate banking roles
- International Banking and Forex Management: correspondent banking operations, FOREX treasury management, and trade finance instrument application for international banking and treasury roles
- Risk Management: credit risk, market risk, operational risk, and liquidity risk measurement and management frameworks for risk management and audit roles

**Applied skills:**
- Investment Banking: DCF and comparable company valuation, M&A deal structuring, and capital market transaction management for investment banking and corporate finance roles
- Management of Financial Services: mutual fund operations, insurance product management, and NBFC service delivery for financial services management

**Soft skills:** Presenting credit proposals to credit committees, negotiating loan terms with corporate borrowers, building investment banking pitch books, and communicating risk exposure to senior banking management.

comparisons.body:
Comparing LPU Online MBA Banking and Financial Services against peers:

**vs Amity Online MBA Finance and Accounting Management:** Amity is NAAC A+ and NIRF #22 at Rs 2,25,000. Amity Finance and Accounting Management is a strong all-India brand with coverage of banking operations and financial markets. LPU Banking and Financial Services at Rs 1,61,600 with grant is significantly more affordable and adds dedicated Investment Banking and Corporate Banking subjects. For North India BFSI careers on a budget with zero-cost EMI, LPU is a strong value proposition.

**vs MUJ Online MBA Finance (BFSI electives):** MUJ Finance covers some BFSI topics through Finance subjects and Coursera Plus certifications but does not offer a dedicated Banking and Financial Services specialisation. LPU's dedicated BFSI specialisation with Corporate Banking, Investment Banking, and Management of Financial Services subjects provides deeper banking-specific content.

**vs NMIMS Online MBA Financial Management:** NMIMS Financial Management is Rs 1,60,000-1,95,000 and carries strong Mumbai BFSI brand recognition. For Mumbai investment banking and BFSI careers, NMIMS carries stronger brand weight. For North India banking and NBFC careers, LPU Banking and Financial Services at Rs 1,61,600 is a comparable and more accessible alternative.

fees.body:
The LPU Online MBA in Banking and Financial Services offers 2 fee tracks:

**Track 1 - Standard Fee**:
- Registration fee: Rs 1,000 (one-time)
- Total program fee: Rs 2,00,000
- Semester fee: Rs 50,000 per semester across 4 semesters

**Track 2 - With 20% Student Grant**:
- Registration fee: Rs 1,000 (one-time)
- Total program fee: Rs 1,61,600 after 20% Student Grant
- Semester fee: Rs 40,400 per semester

**Zero-Cost EMI option (Track 2)**:
- 24-month zero-cost EMI: Rs 6,733 per month
- No interest charged. No processing fee from the university.
- EMI is routed through the university's marketing partner.

The 20% Student Grant is applied at admission. Confirm current grant eligibility and applicable conditions with our counsellor before applying.

EdifyEdu reconfirms fees with LPU each quarter. Please reconfirm current fees and grant eligibility with our counsellor before any payment. Fees listed are indicative.

certificate.body: The degree certificate is issued by Lovely Professional University (LPU) and carries the LPU name with the MBA designation. The Banking and Financial Services specialisation is noted on the marksheet and transcript. Online mode is identified per UGC-DEB 2020 regulations. The certificate is valid for corporate hiring, PSU eligibility, and higher education admission across India.

reviews.intro: Three verified reviews from LPU Online MBA Banking and Financial Services alumni. Ratings from post-completion surveys.

items.name: Navdeep Arora
items.liked: Corporate Banking and Financial Statement Analysis subjects were directly applicable to my credit analyst role at a PSU bank. Risk Management gave me formal frameworks for work I was doing intuitively.
items.disliked: Investment Banking content could be more India-specific with more SEBI and DRHP-related case studies.
items.body: I had 5 years in bank credit and the LPU BFSI MBA helped me move to credit manager level. The Rs 1.61L fee with zero-cost EMI was the key factor. LPU brand is well recognised at North India PSU and private banks.

items.name: Ritika Bhardwaj
items.liked: Management of Financial Services and International Banking subjects were excellent for my NBFC relationship role.
items.disliked: Investment Banking subject could have more practical deal simulation exercises.
items.body: Good programme for BFSI career advancement. Delhi banking companies recognised the LPU credential. The zero-cost EMI made it very accessible for a working banker on a monthly salary.

items.name: Amandeep Singh
items.liked: Risk Management and Corporate Banking subjects were strong and directly applicable.
items.disliked: Placement support for investment banking roles was limited outside major metros.
items.body: Strong BFSI content at a very affordable price. For Punjab and North India banking careers, LPU is well recognised. For Mumbai investment banking, NMIMS would carry stronger brand weight.

faqs.question: How does the LPU 20% Student Grant work?
faqs.answer: The 20% Student Grant reduces the standard Rs 2,00,000 fee to Rs 1,61,600. It is applied at admission. Confirm current grant eligibility conditions with our counsellor before applying, as grant availability may vary by intake cycle.

faqs.question: Is LPU Online MBA zero-cost EMI genuinely interest-free?
faqs.answer: Yes. The 24-month zero-cost EMI at Rs 6,733 per month carries no interest charge and no processing fee from the university. It is routed through the university's marketing partner. Confirm current EMI terms before committing.

faqs.question: Is LPU Banking MBA suitable for PSU bank officers?
faqs.answer: Yes. Financial Statement Analysis and Credit Management, Corporate Banking, and Risk Management subjects are directly aligned with PSU bank credit, relationship management, and risk officer roles. The LPU degree is UGC-DEB approved and valid for departmental promotion eligibility where a UGC-recognised MBA is required.

faqs.question: Does LPU Banking MBA cover fintech and digital banking?
faqs.answer: Core Sem 1-2 subjects cover Information Systems for Business and Corporate Finance which include digital banking context. The Banking and Financial Services Sem 3-4 subjects focus on traditional banking, credit, and investment banking operations. Students specifically targeting fintech management may also consider LPU's Information Technology or Business Analytics specialisations.

faqs.question: Is LPU Online MBA NIRF ranked?
faqs.answer: Yes. Lovely Professional University is ranked NIRF #31 (University category, 2025) and NIRF #44 in the Management category. This places LPU among the top-ranked universities offering UGC-DEB approved online MBAs in India.

chooseIf: You are a working banking professional targeting credit management, corporate banking, risk management, or financial services management roles.
You want a UGC-DEB approved MBA from a NIRF #31 university at Rs 1,61,600 (with grant) or Rs 2,00,000 standard.
You want 24-month zero-cost EMI at Rs 6,733 per month with no interest charge.
consider: If Mumbai investment banking or BFSI brand recognition matters, NMIMS Online MBA Financial Management carries stronger recognition in those markets.
If pan-India BFSI brand recognition is the priority, Amity Online MBA Banking and Finance at Rs 2.25L is well recognised.
If a pure Finance specialisation covering derivatives and portfolio management is preferred, LPU's Finance specialisation covers those topics more broadly.
